questions.q: The mean and variance of a data set comprising <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> respectively. If one of the observation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> is deleted and two new observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> are added to the data, then the new variance of resulting data is
questions.solution:
Let 15 observations are x1,x2……x15

x1+x2+x3……x1515=15

If x15=15

x¯new=x1+x2+x3…x15-15+6+816=22416=14

Σxi215-x¯2=5 ⇒ x12+x22……x142+152=230×15

⇒  x12+x22……x142=3225

New variance = x12+x22……x142+62+8216-x¯new2

=332516-196

= 11.8125
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If the standard deviation of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>k</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
S.D.=∑xi2n-∑xin2=5

⇒∑xi2n-∑xin2=5

⇒1+1+0+k24--1+0+1+k42=5

⇒2+k24-k216=5 

⇒8+4k2-k216=5

⇒8+3k2=80

⇒3k2=72 

⇒k2=24 

⇒k=±26

Since, k>0

 ⇒k=26.

questions.q:
If a variable takes values 0, 1, 2,...,n with frequencies 2n , C1 n·pqn-1, nC2p2·qn-1,………, nCnpn respectively,  then the mean of the distribution is 

(where p+q=1).
questions.solution:
∴x¯=0·qn+1·n1qn-1p+2·nn-12!qn-2p2+...+n·pnqn+n1qn-1p+nn-12qn-2p2+...+pn

       =0·nC0qnp0+1·nC1qn-1p+...+nnCnq0pnnC0qnp0+nC1qn-1p1+...+nCnqn-npn

       =Σr=0nrn·Crqn-rprΣr=0nnCrqn-rpr

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In ten observations, the mean of all <math><mn>10</mn></math> numbers is <math><mn>15</mn></math>, the mean of the first six observations is <math><mn>16</mn></math> and the mean of the last five observations is <math><mn>12</mn></math>. The sixth number is
questions.solution:
Let the mean of the last four observations be A2. Then, by the formula for combined mean, we get,

15=6×16+4×A26+4 

or 150=96+4A2 

∴A2=544

Let the sixth number is x, then taking the sixth number as a collection, the combined mean of this collection and the collection of the last four is 12.

∴ By the definition of combined mean

12=1×x+4×5441+4

∴60=x+54∴x=6

Hence, the sixth number =6
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If 1 is added to first 10 natural numbers, then variance of the numbers so obtained is
questions.solution:
We have to find variance of numbers $2,3,4, \ldots ., 11$.
Here $\overline{\mathrm{x}}=\frac{2+3+4+\ldots+11}{10}=\frac{65}{10}=6.5$
Variance $=\frac{\sum\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\overline{\mathrm{x}}\right)^2}{\mathrm{n}}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{\left[(-4.5)^2+(-3.5)^2+(-2.5)^2+(-1.5)^2+(-0.5)^2+(0.5)^2+(1.5)^2+(2.5)^2+(3.5)^2+(4.5)^2\right]}{10} \\
& =\frac{2(20.25+12.25+6.25+2.25+0.25)}{10}=\frac{41.25}{5}=8.25
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the sum of mean and variance of a binomial distribution for 5 trials is 1.8 , then probability of a success is
questions.solution:
We have $n p+n p q=1.8$, where $n=5$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \mathrm{np}(1+\mathrm{q})=1.8 \Rightarrow 5 \mathrm{p}[1+(1-\mathrm{p})]=1.8 \\
& \therefore 5 \mathrm{p}(2-\mathrm{p})=1.8 \Rightarrow 10 \mathrm{p}-5 \mathrm{p}^2=1.8 \text { i.e. } \\
& 5 \mathrm{p}^2-10 \mathrm{p}+1.8=0 \Rightarrow 5 \mathrm{p}^2-9 \mathrm{p}-\mathrm{p}+1.8=0 \\
& \therefore 5 \mathrm{p}(\mathrm{p}-1.8)-1(\mathrm{p}-1.8)=0 \Rightarrow(5 \mathrm{p}-1)(\mathrm{p}-1.8)=0 \\
& \therefore \mathrm{p}=\frac{1}{5}, 1.8 \text { but } \mathrm{p} \leq 1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{p}=\frac{1}{5}=0.2
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If mean deviation about the mean of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>6</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>8</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>200</mn><mi>λ</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math>, the median of the given numbers can be
questions.solution:
Given data is 2λ, 4λ, 6λ, 8λ, ....., 200λ

Mean, x¯=2λ+4λ+6λ+.....+200λ100=2λ·10021+100100

x¯=101λ

Given, mean deviation about mean is 100

i.e. 100=∑i=1100xi-x¯100

⇒  10000=99λ+97λ+95λ+.....+λ

+λ+3λ+5λ+....+99λ

⇒  2λ.1+3+5+....+99=10000

⇒  2λ.502×100=10000

⇒  λ=2

⇒  λ=±2

Now median of 2λ, 4λ, 6λ,....200λ is 100λ+102λ2

⇒  Median=101λ=±202

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For two data sets each of size 5 , the variance are given to be 4 and 5 and the corresponding means are given to be 2 and 4 respectively. The variance of the combined data set is
questions.solution:
We have $\mathrm{n}_1=5, \sigma_1{ }^2=4, \overline{\mathrm{x}}_1=2$ and $\mathrm{n}_2=5, \sigma_2{ }^2=5, \overline{\mathrm{x}}_2=4$
here combined mean $=\bar{x}_c=\frac{(5)(2)+(5)(4)}{(5)+(5)}=3$
$$
\mathrm{d}_1=\overline{\mathrm{x}}_1-\overline{\mathrm{x}}_{\mathrm{c}}=2-3=1 \text { and } \mathrm{d}_2=\overline{\mathrm{x}}_2-\overline{\mathrm{x}}_{\mathrm{c}}=4-3=1
$$
Combined variance $=$
$$
=\frac{\mathrm{n}_1\left(\sigma_1^2+\mathrm{d}_1^2\right)+\mathrm{n}_2\left(\sigma_2^2+\mathrm{d}_2^2\right)}{\mathrm{n}_1+\mathrm{n}_2}=\frac{5(4+1)+5(5+1)}{5+5}=\frac{11}{2}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha \neq 0$ and the mean deviation of the observations $\{k \alpha\}$, for $k=1,2, \ldots \ldots, 50$ about its median is 50 , then $|\alpha|=$
questions.solution:
Given observations $=\{\mathrm{K} \alpha\}$
Here, $\mathrm{K}=1,2,3, \ldots \ldots, 50$
$$
\therefore \quad\{\mathrm{K} \alpha\}=\{\alpha, 2 \alpha, 3 \alpha, \ldots . ., 50 \alpha\}
$$
Number of observations $=50$ (Even)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Median of given observation }=\frac{25 \alpha+26 \alpha}{2} \\
& M=25.5 \alpha
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, mean deviation about median
$\begin{aligned} & \text { M,D. (M) }=\frac{\sum_{k=1}^n\left|x_i-m\right|}{n} \\ & 50=\frac{\sum_{k=1}^{50}|k \alpha-25.5 \alpha|}{50} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 50 \times 50=\sum_{k=1}^{50}|\alpha(K-25.5)| \\ & \Rightarrow \quad|\alpha| \sum_{k=1}^{50}|k-25.5|=2500 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2|\alpha|[24.5+23.5+. .+1.5+0.5]=2500 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2|\alpha| \times \frac{25}{2} \times(24.5+0.5)=2500 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad|\alpha| \times 25=1400 \Rightarrow|\alpha|=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If for some x∈R, the frequency distribution of the marks obtained by 20 students in a test is:

Marks 2 3 5 7
Frequency distribution x+12 2x-5 x2-3x x

Then the mean of the marks is :
questions.solution:
Total students =20

⇒  x+12+2x-5+x2-3x+x=20

⇒   x2+2x+1+2x-5+x2-3x+x=20

⇒   2x2+2x-24=0  ⇒  x2+x-12=0

⇒    x=-4, 3    ∵  x>0

∴    x=3

Average =x+12×2+2x-53+5x2-3x+7×x20

=32+3+0+2120=2.8

Hence, required mean is 2.8
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The marks obtained by students $A$ and $B$ in 3 examinations are given below
The ratio of the coefficient of variation of marks of $A$ and the coefficient of variation of marks of $B$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text {  Marks of } A=30,20,40 \\ & \bar{x}_A=\frac{30+20+40}{3}=30 \\ & \sigma_A=\sqrt{\frac{0^2+(-10)^2+(10)^2}{3}}=\sqrt{\frac{200}{3}} \\ & {\left[\sigma=\sqrt{\frac{\left(x_i-\bar{x}\right)^2}{n}}\right.}\end{aligned}$
$$
(C V)_A=\sqrt{\frac{200}{3}} \times \frac{1}{30}=\frac{10 \sqrt{2}}{30 \sqrt{3}}=\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3 \sqrt{3}} \quad\left[C V=\frac{\sigma}{\bar{x}}\right]
$$
Now, marks of $B=70,0,5$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\bar{x}_B & =\frac{70+0+5}{3}=25 \\
\sigma_B & =\sqrt{\frac{(70-25)^2+(0-25)^2+(5-25)^2}{3}} \\
\sigma_B & =\sqrt{\frac{3050}{3}} \\
(C V)_B & =\sqrt{\frac{3050}{3}} \times \frac{1}{25} \\
& =\frac{5}{25} \sqrt{\frac{122}{3}}=\frac{1}{5} \sqrt{\frac{122}{3}} \\
(C V)_A:(C V)_B & =\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3 \sqrt{3}}: \frac{1}{5} \sqrt{\frac{122}{3}} \\
& \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3 \sqrt{3}}: \frac{1}{5} \frac{\sqrt{2} \sqrt{61}}{\sqrt{3}}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math>. If each observation is multiplied by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> is subtracted from it, then the variance of the new data is
questions.solution:
Let the observations be x1, x2, x3,…,x50 and the mean be x¯.

From the given data,

Variance =7, the number of observations n=50.

Variance =1n∑xi-x¯2

⇒7=150∑xi-x¯2

⇒350=∑xi-x¯2  ...1 

Each observation is multiplied by 6 and then 5 is subtracted.

Let the new observations be y1, y2, y3,…,y50.

yi=6xi-5

xi=yi+56  ...2 

The new variance =1n∑yi-y¯2

y¯=1n∑yi  ...3 

⇒y¯=150∑6xi-5

⇒y¯=150∑6xi-∑050150·5

⇒y¯=150∑6xi-5

⇒y¯=6x¯-5   [∴ From 1]

⇒x¯=y¯+56  ...4 

⇒∑xi-x¯2=350

⇒∑yi+56-y¯+562=350  [∴ From 2 & 4]

⇒∑162yi-y¯2=350

⇒∑yi-y¯2=36×350

⇒∑yi-y¯2=12600

New variance=150∑yi-y¯2=150×12600=252
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The mean and variance of <math><mn>20</mn></math> observations are found to be <math><mn>10</mn></math> and <math><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi></math> respectively. On rechecking, it was found that an observation <math><mn>9</mn></math> was incorrect and the correct observation was <math><mn>11,</mn></math> then the correct variance is
questions.solution:
∑xi20=10    … (i)
∑xi220-100=4      … (ii)
∑xi2=104×20=2080
Actual mean =200-9+1120=20220
Variance =2080-81+12120-202202
=212020-10.12=106-102.01=3.99

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>x</mi><mo>¯</mo></mover></math> is the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msub></math> then the mean of the absolute deviations of these observations from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>x</mi><mo>¯</mo></mover></math> is
options: the mean proportion of the data
the standard deviation of the data
the mean deviation of the data
questions.solution:
If x¯ is the mean of n observations x1,x2,…,xn then the mean of the absolute deviations of these observations from x¯ is the mean deviation of the data.

Here,

x¯=x1+x2+...+xnn    ....1

Mean deviation

=x1-x¯+x2-x¯+...+xn-x¯n
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A): The variance of the first $\mathrm{n}$ odd natural numbers is $\frac{n^2-1}{3}$.

Reason (R): The sum of the first $\mathrm{n}$ odd natural numbers is $\mathrm{n}^2$ and the sum of the squares of the first $\mathrm{n}$ odd natural numbers is $\frac{n\left(4 n^2-1\right)}{3}$.

Which of the following alternatives is correct?
options: (A) and (R) are true. (R) is correct explanation of (A)
(A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not a correct explanation of $(\mathrm{A})$
questions.solution:
Sum of first $n$ odd natural numbers
$$
1+3+5+\ldots . .+(2 n-1)=\frac{n}{2}(1+2 n-1)=n^2
$$

Sum of squares of first $n$ odd natural numbers
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\sum(2 n-1)^2=\sum\left(4 n^2+1-4 n\right) \\
& =\frac{4 n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6}-\frac{4 n(n+1)}{2}+n \\
& =\frac{2 n(n+1)(2 n+1)-6 n(n+1)+n}{3} \\
& =\frac{n}{3}\left(4 n^2+6 n+2-6 n-6+3\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{n}{3}\left(4 n^2-1\right) \\
& \text { Variance }=\frac{\Sigma x_i^2}{n}-\left(\frac{\Sigma x_i}{n}\right)^2 \\
& =\frac{n}{3 n}\left(4 n^2-1\right)-\left(\frac{n^2}{n}\right)^2 \\
& =\frac{4 n^2-1}{3}-n^2=\frac{n^2-1}{3}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore A$ and $R$ are true. $R$ is correct explanation of $A$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If mean and standard deviation of <math><mn>5</mn></math> observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> and <math><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo></math> respectively, then the variance of <math><mn>6</mn></math> observations <math><msub><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mo>-</mo><mn>50</mn></math> is equal to:
questions.solution:
We have,

Mean =x1+x2+x3+x4+x55=10⇒x1+x2+x3+x4+x5=50  ...i 

Variance=Σxi25-x¯2=S.D2=9 

⇒x12+x22+x32+x42+x525-100=9

⇒x12+x22+x32+x42+x52=545  ...ii 

Now, x¯new=x1+x2+x3+x4+x5-506=0 

New variance =∑i=16xi26-x¯new2 

=x12+x22+x32+x42+x52+25006-0

=545+25006=30456=507.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The standard deviation of the numbers 22, $26,28,20,24,30$ is
questions.solution:
Given numbers 22, 26, 28, 20, 24, 30
$$
\bar{x}=\frac{22+26+28+20+24+30}{6}=\frac{150}{6}=25
$$
Now, $\left(x_i-\bar{x}\right)^2$ is $9,1,9,25,1,25$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Now, } S D=\sqrt{\frac{\Sigma\left(x_i-\bar{x}\right)^2}{n}} \\
& =\sqrt{\frac{9+1+9+25+1+25}{6}}=\sqrt{\frac{70}{6}}=3.42
\end{aligned}
$$

Arranging the observations in ascending order of magnitude, we have 150,210,240,300,310,320,340 clearly, the middle observation is 300. So, median =300

Mean deviation=1n∑di=17∑xi-300=3707=52.8

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Coefficient of variation of two distributions are <math><mn>60</mn><mi>%</mi></math> and <math><mn>75</mn><mi>%</mi><mo>,</mo></math> and their standard deviation are <math><mn>18</mn></math> and <math><mn>15</mn></math> respectively, then their arithmetic means respectively are
questions.solution:
Given, CV1=60,CV2=75,σ1=18 and σ2=15
Let x-1 and x-2 be the means of 1st and 2nd distribution respectively.
Then, CV1=σ1x-1×100⇒x-1=18×10060=30
CV2=σ2x-2×100⇒x-2=15×10075=20
Hence, x-1=30 and x-2=20
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let one observation equal to the mean is added to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> observations. If the variance changes from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>78</mn></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>72</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Adding mean to the observations does not change the value of ∑xi-x-2, hence, 

∑i=1nxi-x-2n∑i=1n+1xi-x-2n+1=7872 
⇒n+1n=7872⇒n=12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The mean of five numbers is <math><mn>0</mn></math> and their variance is <math><mi></mi><mn>2</mn></math> . If three of those numbers are <math><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mn>1</mn></math> and <math><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the other two numbers are
questions.solution:
Let the other two numbers be x and y.
According to the question,
Mean=-1+1+2+x+y5=0⇒x+y=-2…i
Also, σ2=2
⇒-1-02+1-02+2-02+x-02+y-025=2
⇒1+1+4+x2+y2=10⇒x2+y2=4…ii
⇒x+y2-2xy=4
⇒4-2xy=4⇒xy=0…iii
Now, x-y2=x2+y2-2xy=4-0=4 {using ii and iii } …iv
⇒x-y=±2
Solving i and iv , we get,
If x-y=2,x=0,y=-2
If x-y=-2,x=-2,y=0
So, the other two numbers are -2,0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If a variable takes the values as 0, 1, 2,....n with frequencies as<sup> </sup><sup>n</sup>C<sub>0</sub>, <sup>n</sup>C<sub>1</sub>, <sup>n</sup>C<sub>2</sub>, ....., <sup>n</sup>C<sub>n</sub> respectively, then mean and variance are given as :
options: <math><mrow>  <mfrac>    <mrow>      <mi>n</mi>      <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">        <mrow>          <mi>n</mi>          <mo>+</mo>          <mn>1</mn>        </mrow>      </mfenced>    </mrow>    <mrow>      <mn>2</mn>    </mrow>  </mfrac>  <mtext>,</mtext>  <mfrac>    <mrow>      <mi>n</mi>      <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">        <mrow>          <mi>n</mi>          <mo>+</mo>          <mn>1</mn>        </mrow>      </mfenced>      <mfenced open="(" close=")" separators=",">        <mrow>          <mn>2</mn>          <mi>n</mi>          <mo>+</mo>          <mn>1</mn>        </mrow>      </mfenced>    </mrow>    <mrow>      <mn>6</mn>    </mrow>  </mfrac></mrow></math>
questions.solution:
We know that x ¯ = ∑ f⁡ x ∑ f⁡ and σ = ∑ f⁡ x 2 ∑ f⁡ - x ¯ 2

Here Σ f = 1 + nC1 + nC2 + ... +n. nCn = 2n

Σ f x = 0.1 + 1 . nC1 + 2. nC2 +3. nC3 +...+n.nCn = n.2n-1 

​
Σfx2  = 0.1 + 12.nC1 + 22. nC2 +  32. nC3 + ... + n2.nCn = n 2n-1 + n (n-1) 2n-2 

TO obtain the above results, let  

1 + y 2 = 1 + n C 1 y + n C 2 y 2 + … … + n C n y n … i
put y = 1,

2n = 1 + nC1 + nC2 + ... + nCn = Σf 
Differentiating (i) with resect to (y), we get 


n (1 + y) n-1 = nC1 + nC2 (2y) + nC3 (3y2) + ... + nCn (nyn-1)

​put y =1 

n2n-1 = nC1 + nC2 (2) + nC3 (3) +..+nCn n = Σ f

again  ∑ f⁡ x 2 = ∑ r = 0 n r 2 n C r⁡ = ∑ r = 0 n r r - 1 + r n ! r ! n - r !

= ∑ r r - 1 n ! r ! n - r ! + r n ! r ! n - r !

= ∑ n ! ( r - 2 ) ! n - r ! + ∑ rn ! r - 1 ! n - r !

= n n - 1 n - 2 ! ( r - 2 ) ! n - r ! + n ∑ n - 1 ! r - 1 ! n - r !

= n(n-1) Σn-2Cr-2 + n Σn-1Cr-1

n (n - 1) 2n-2 + n 2n-1

x ¯ = ∑ f⁡ x ∑ f⁡ = n 2 n - 1 2 n = n 2

σ 2 = n n - 1 - 2 n - 2 + n 2 n - 1 2 n - n 2 2 = n 4
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The algebraic sum of the deviation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> observations measured from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math>. Then, mean of observations is
questions.solution:
Given that,  Σi= 120xi-30=2

⇒ Σi= 120xi-Σi= 12030=2

⇒ x¯=20·3020+220

= 30+0.1=30.1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Following data shows the information about marks obtained in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics and Biology by 100 students in a class. Then
subject shows the highest variability in marks
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { We know that Coefficient of Variation }=\frac{\text { Standard Deviation }}{\text { mean }} \\ & \therefore \quad(\text { C.V. })_{\text {Physics }}=\frac{3}{20}=0.15 \text { and }(\text { C.V. })_{\text {Chemistry }}=\frac{2}{25}=0.08 \\ & \left.\text { (C.V. })_{\text {Mathematics }}=\frac{4}{23}=0.174 \text { and (C.V. }\right)_{\text {Biology }}=\frac{5}{27}=0.185\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The arithmetic mean of marks in Mathematics for four divisions A, B, C and D were 80, 75, 70 and 72 respectively. Their standard deviations were $12,6,8$ and and 10 respectively. Then, division
has more uniformity.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { We know that C.V. }=\frac{\text { Standard Deviation }}{\text { Mean }} \\
& \therefore(\text { C.V. })_A=\frac{12}{18}=0.15 \text { and }(\text { C.V. })_B=\frac{6}{75}=0.08 \\
& (\text { C.V. })_C=\frac{8}{70}=0.11 \text { and }(\text { C.V. })_D=\frac{10}{72}=0.14
\end{aligned}
$$
C.V. is the least for division B.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If $m$ and $\sigma^2$ are the mean and variance of the random variable $X$, whose distribution is given by
Then
questions.solution:
Given, distribution is
$\begin{aligned}\therefore Mean, m & =\sum_{i=1}^4 p_i x_i \\ & =0 \times \frac{1}{3}+1 \times \frac{1}{2}+2 \times 0+3 \times \frac{1}{6}\end{aligned}$ $=0+\frac{1}{2}+0+\frac{1}{2}=1$ Variance, $\sigma^2=\sum_{i=1}^4 p_i\left(x_i-m\right)^2$ $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{3}(0-1)^2+\frac{1}{2}(1-1)^2 \\ & \quad+0(2-1)^2+\frac{1}{6}(3-1)^2 \\ & =\frac{1}{3}+0+0+\frac{2}{3}=1 \\ & m=\sigma^2=1 \end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad m=\sigma^2=1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Let x¯, M and σ2 be respectively the mean, mode and variance of n observations x1, x2,...., xn and di=-xi-a, i=1, 2,...., n, where a is any number.

Statement I: Variance of d1, d2,..., dn is σ 2 .

Statement II: Mean and mode of d1, d2,...., dn are -x¯-a and -M-a, respectively.
options: Statement I and Statement II are both true
Statement I and Statement II are both false
Statement I is true and Statement II is false
Statement I is false and Statement II is true
questions.solution:
Mean of d1, d2, d3... dn

=∑  -xi-a n 

=-x→-a

And, the mode of d1, d2, d3... dn will be -M-a

Also, we know that the variance σ2 is unaffected by change of origin, hence it remains same.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
